# Skill Routing

No embedding search, no keyword matching — the LLM itself decides which skills to load.

![architecture-06](diagrams/architecture-06.svg)

---

## SKILL.md Format

```yaml
---
name: scheduled_crons
description: >
  Create, modify, and delete scheduled cron jobs.
  Use when scheduling recurring tasks on a cron.
  Do NOT use for one-time tasks (use create_thread instead).
---
# Full instructions below (up to 500 lines)
# Code examples, anti-patterns, SDK reference...

<!-- END OF AUTOGENERATED CONTENT -->
# Custom agent-written content preserved across SDK updates
```

---

## Three Iterations of Tool Discovery

| Version | Approach | Outcome |
|---------|----------|---------|
| v1 | Dump all tool schemas into context | Abandoned — too wasteful |
| v2 | Search-based discovery | Abandoned — agents don't know what they don't know |
| **v3** | **One-line summaries + lazy loading** | **Current — ~68 lines = ~200 tokens** |

---

## API Reference — Documentation Lookup Tools

Two tools provide runtime access to external library/API documentation via Context7.

**Source:** `sdk/tools/docs_tools.py`

### `resolve_library_id`

Resolves a library/framework/API name to a Context7-compatible ID. Use this first before `query_library_docs`.

```json
{
  "role": "resolve_library_id",
  "arguments": {
    "library_name": "stripe api",
    "query": "how to create a payment intent"
  }
}
```

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `library_name` | `string` | Yes | Library, framework, API, or service name (e.g. `"react"`, `"stripe api"`, `"postgresql"`) |
| `query` | `string` | Yes | Question/task — used to rank results by relevance |

**Response:**

```json
{
  "result": {
    "library_id": "/stripe/stripe-node",
    "library_name": "Stripe Node.js",
    "description": "Stripe API client for Node.js",
    "alternatives": [
      {"library_id": "/stripe/stripe-python", "library_name": "Stripe Python", "description": "..."}
    ]
  }
}
```

### `query_library_docs`

Fetches relevant documentation and code examples for a resolved library ID.

```json
{
  "role": "query_library_docs",
  "arguments": {
    "library_id": "/stripe/stripe-node",
    "query": "How to create a payment intent with metadata"
  }
}
```

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `library_id` | `string` | Yes | Context7 ID from `resolve_library_id` (e.g. `"/vercel/next.js"`) |
| `query` | `string` | Yes | Specific question — be detailed for better results |

**Response:**

```json
{
  "result": {
    "library_id": "/stripe/stripe-node",
    "documentation": "## Creating a PaymentIntent\n\n```javascript\nconst paymentIntent = await stripe.paymentIntents.create({\n  amount: 2000,\n  currency: 'usd',\n  metadata: { order_id: '123' }\n});\n```\n..."
  }
}
```

## API Reference — Integration Connect URLs

Utility for generating OAuth connection URLs with thread auto-reactivation.

**Source:** `sdk/utils/integrations.py`

```python
from sdk.utils.integrations import get_integration_connect_url

url = get_integration_connect_url("linear")
# Returns: "https://app.getviktor.com/integrations/linear?team=xxx&source_thread_id=yyy"
```

The `source_thread_id` parameter causes the platform to automatically reactivate the agent's thread after the user completes the OAuth flow. Integration slugs are stored in `/work/sdk/docs/available_integrations.json` (one JSON object per line).

---

*Sources: `sdk/tools/docs_tools.py`, `sdk/utils/integrations.py`, `skills/skill_creation/SKILL.md`, [Viktor blog: What Breaks When Your Agent Has 100,000 Tools](https://getviktor.com/blog/what-breaks-when-your-agent-has-100000-tools)*
